grants.strategy_note: Focus on zero-age-requirement funders now. Program development (Youth Mentoring, Sobriety & Recovery, Women's Empowerment) unlocks major grant categories. 2-year minimum funders go on calendar for May 2027.

pipeline.funder: Bank of America Empowering Communities RFP
pipeline.notes: URGENT — deadline May 18. Register on NPO Hub before applying. No age requirement. Position: food security + family strengthening.
Rolling basis, no deadline. No age requirement. Community Opportunity + Local Resiliency angle.
Gift cards for food pantry supply purchases. Low barrier, quick turnaround. No age requirement.
No age requirement. Hunger + Health focus. Quarterly windows: Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct. Prepare now for July.
Youth 14–24, school-to-career programs. No age requirement. Tampa Bay NBA market qualifies. Youth Mentoring must be operational before applying.

pipeline.area: Food Security / Education & Youth / Family Strengthening
pipeline.notes: No age requirement found. Childhood hunger + youth education + family services. $6M awarded to 56 orgs in 2026 cycle.
Youth sports development. Basketball for Health Promotion is a natural fit. Girls flag football expansion initiative active.
Requires 1 x 990 filing — now satisfied. Best local fit. 5 focus areas map exactly to iCARE's. START RELATIONSHIP NOW. Apply Dec 2026.
Requires 1+ year operating — now satisfied. Youth empowerment + community engagement. Apply 2027 cycle.
ACE Capacity Grant. No age requirement. 75%+ clients in ALICE focus areas required — begin tracking participant data now.
No age requirement. 1:1 match required (in-kind OK — $14K+ already documented). Strong food pantry case.
Sobriety & Recovery program must be operational before applying.
Youth development / life skills. Basketball for Health Promotion angle.
Top-priority federal grant. Requires SAM.gov registration (not done). Couples + Family Support programs must be documented.

pipeline.funder: SAMHSA (via FL Dept of Children & Families)
pipeline.notes: Direct match to Sobriety & Recovery program (in development). No age requirement at state level. Program must launch first.
Mentoring program must be operational. SAM.gov required. Heavy competitive process. Target 2027.
Federal poverty block grant distributed through FL state office.
EXPLICIT 2-year minimum. iCARE's own bank — highest priority once eligible May 2027.
Requires 2 years audited financials. DO NOT apply before May 2027.
Age requirement not explicitly stated — verify before applying. High fit for Youth Mentoring + Back-to-School.
Conservative funder, prefers established orgs. Tampa Bay local. Start relationship in 2026.
Likely 2-year minimum. Women's Empowerment program must also be operational.
DENIED April 2026. Requires 4+ years of operation. iCARE is ~1 year old. Do not reapply until May 2029.

strategy.vision: Become the premier Two-Generation family-strengthening nonprofit in Tampa Bay — and a model for what a lean, community-rooted 501(c)(3) can accomplish.
strategy.geographic_focus: Tampa Bay / Hillsborough County primary. International aspirations long-term — to be confirmed as the organization matures.
strategy.model: Two-Generation (Two-Gen): we serve children and their parents simultaneously across 5 interconnected program areas. This is our competitive differentiator with funders.
strategy.why_it_matters_for_board: Every program we run, every grant we apply for, every dollar we document — all of it builds a 3-year track record. Funders evaluate the board as credibility. Board members are not just governance — you are the foundation that unlocks funding.
strategy.grant_eligibility_rule: MANDATORY: All grants require an eligibility pre-flight check before drafting. Minimum operating history, geography, and program fit must be verified. Publix was denied due to 4-year requirement — this check is non-negotiable.

strategy.program_priority_rationale: Food Security (#1) has the most documented 2025 activity and strongest immediate grant match. Family Strengthening (#2) unlocks the largest long-term federal funding. Education & Youth (#3), Health & Wellness (#4), and Career Development (#5) build the Two-Gen case across all applications.
what_board_members_control: Vote on all major decisions (4 of 5 required for bylaws, compensation, dissolution)
Support annual IRS filing (Form 990-N) — Treasurer reviews financials, President certifies
Authorize the President as AOR on every grant application
Attend and document attendance — quorum (3 of 5) required for valid votes
Sign annual Conflict of Interest disclosures — required by most grantors
